commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=fc7007e82b983bbdbc77818ef877eb767b8a3ac7)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a public live provider model-catalog SDK subpath, routes bundled provider plugins through it, changes OpenAI/OpenCode Go discovery modes, and updates docs, tests, exports, and API metadata.

PR surface: Source +481, Tests +560, Docs +142, Config +7, Generated 0, Other +10. Total +1200 across 37 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection of the PR head shows OpenAI and xAI expose both live and static catalog hooks while `loadProviderCatalogModelsForList` takes the static branch whenever `staticCatalog` exists.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] This needs contributor-provided live proof plus maintainer acceptance of the new SDK/fallback contract; automation cannot supply the missing real provider proof.

target.title: feat: add live provider model catalog helper
target.body:
## Summary
- Add and extend `openclaw/plugin-sdk/provider-catalog-live-runtime` for guarded live `/models` discovery, raw-row projection, structured HTTP fallback, auth-scoped bounded TTL caching, and test cache cleanup through the public subpath.
- Route bundled live-discovery providers through the SDK path: OpenCode Go, OpenAI, xAI, Chutes, Venice, DeepInfra, Vercel AI Gateway, and NVIDIA.
- Keep provider-owned static metadata as the source of capabilities, pricing, media limits, endpoint family, and offline fallback rows; preserve manifest `mediaInput` when converting manifest catalogs.
- Keep OpenAI live/static catalogs scoped to the `openai` provider id instead of expanding through Azure hook aliases, and mark OpenCode Go manifest discovery as runtime-owned so the normal provider-discovery path reaches the live hook.
- Update plugin-builder docs, SDK subpath docs, API metadata, and the tracked SDK API baseline hash for the live discovery route.

## How it works
Provider plugins keep their static catalog rows as the canonical metadata and fallback. In `catalog.run`, a provider can call `buildLiveModelProviderConfig(...)` when the provider API returns live model ids, or `getCachedLiveProviderModelRows(...)` / `fetchLiveProviderModelRows(...)` when the provider needs to project richer API rows into OpenClaw model definitions.

The SDK helper performs an SSRF-guarded fetch, composes caller abort signals with a bounded timeout, builds provider/discovery auth headers, parses OpenAI-style `{ data: [...] }` and top-level array bodies by default, releases guarded fetch resources, caches successful live rows by provider/endpoint/credential with a bounded process-local TTL cache, throws structured `LiveModelCatalogHttpError` for HTTP fallback handling, and falls back to provider-owned static rows when live discovery is unavailable.

OpenAI is now marked as runtime-discovered so API-key users can hit `/v1/models`, while `staticCatalog` keeps the manifest rows available for offline/static browsing and image-compression metadata. OpenAI returns explicit `providers.openai` catalog results so Azure hook aliases remain runtime-hook aliases only. OpenCode Go is marked runtime-discovered so model browsing loads the plugin runtime instead of stopping at the stale two-row manifest catalog. xAI keeps discovery credentials aligned with the returned provider credential. Chutes preserves its bounded token/fallback cache while using the SDK fetch route. DeepInfra, Venice, Vercel AI Gateway, NVIDIA, and OpenCode Go now share the same guarded live-model fetch path while retaining provider-specific parsing.

## Verification
- `OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_SCOPE=worktree node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-sdk/provider-catalog-live-runtime.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/provider-catalog-shared.test.ts extensions/openai/openai-provider.test.ts extensions/xai/index.test.ts extensions/chutes/models.test.ts extensions/venice/models.test.ts extensions/deepinfra/provider-models.test.ts extensions/vercel-ai-gateway/provider-catalog.test.ts extensions/nvidia/provider-catalog.test.ts extensions/opencode-go/index.test.ts src/plugins/provider-discovery.runtime.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/extension-package-project-boundaries.test.ts src/agents/tools/image-tool.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_SCOPE=worktree node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/openai/openai-provider.test.ts src/plugins/provider-discovery.runtime.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_SCOPE=worktree node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/opencode-go/index.test.ts src/plugins/provider-discovery.runtime.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxlint src/agents/tools/image-tool.ts src/plugin-sdk/provider-catalog-shared.ts src/plugin-sdk/provider-catalog-shared.test.ts extensions/chutes/models.test.ts extensions/xai/index.test.ts extensions/openai/openai-provider.test.ts extensions/nvidia/provider-catalog.ts extensions/deepinfra/provider-models.test.ts src/gateway/live-env-test-helpers.ts src/plugin-sdk/qa-runner-runtime.integration.test.ts src/test-utils/env.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/openai/openai-provider.ts extensions/openai/openai-provider.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/opencode-go/index.test.ts scripts/lib/plugin-sdk-doc-metadata.ts`
- `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check`
- `pnpm plugin-sdk:check-exports`
- `pnpm lint:plugins:plugin-sdk-subpaths-exported`
- `OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_SCOPE=worktree pnpm tsgo:core`
- `OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_SCOPE=worktree pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_SCOPE=worktree pnpm tsgo:extensions:test`
- `OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_SCOPE=worktree pnpm build`
- `pnpm docs:list`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` (clean; no accepted/actionable findings)

Known local checkout note: direct `pnpm tsgo:*` and `pnpm build` without `OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_SCOPE=worktree` are blocked by this sandbox because it cannot create `.git/openclaw-local-checks/heavy-check.lock`. The same commands passed with the documented worktree-local lock scope.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior or issue addressed: provider plugins now have a reusable SDK route for live model discovery, and bundled providers with model-list APIs use it without repeated provider-local fetch plumbing or unbounded auth-scoped cache growth.

Real environment tested: local macOS checkout built from this PR branch on June 4, 2026, using the patched built CLI entry `node dist/entry.js`. OpenCode Go runtime provider discovery and browsing were exercised without secrets. OpenAI was exercised through static fallback browsing without printing credentials; authenticated OpenAI/xAI live `/models` calls remain covered by mocked auth-path tests.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
PATH=/Users/jason/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:/Users/jason/.local/bin:$PATH node dist/entry.js models list --provider opencode-go --json
PATH=/Users/jason/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:/Users/jason/.local/bin:$PATH node dist/entry.js models list --provider openai --json
```

Evidence after fix:
```text
opencode-go count: 17
sample runtime provider rows:
- opencode-go/deepseek-v4-flash
- opencode-go/kimi-k2.6
- opencode-go/qwen3.7-max

openai static fallback count: 16
sample fallback rows:
- openai/gpt-5.5 input=text+image contextWindow=1000000 contextTokens=272000 tags=default,configured,alias:gpt
- openai/gpt-5.5-pro input=text+image contextWindow=1000000
```

Observed result after fix: OpenCode Go model browsing returned provider rows after changing its manifest to runtime discovery, while OpenAI static browsing still returned GPT-5.5/GPT-5.5 Pro metadata after changing OpenAI to runtime discovery. Focused tests also prove credential scoping, bounded live-cache eviction, static fallback preservation, media input preservation, provider-specific parsing, and SDK export availability.

What was not tested: authenticated live `/models` requests for OpenAI/xAI and authenticated completion calls for every discovered model were not run because no secrets were used.
target.author: fuller-stack-dev

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
